title: brossaea loxensis
intro: Brossaea loxensis, also known as the 'False Ipecac', is a plant species within the Rubiaceae family, native to tropical regions of South America. Traditionally, Brossaea loxensis is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to induce expectoration and relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to the presence of ipecacuanha alkaloids. The plant's health benefits also include its use as an emetic and anti-inflammatory agent, making it a potential treatment for various gastrointestinal and musculoskeletal conditions. Brossaea loxensis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, often in combination with other medicinal plants, to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
